title: podman does not transfer ownership/permissions of underlying directory to volume
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When using docker, mounting a volume onto a directory in the container will copy the ownership and permissions of the mountpoint onto the volume.  That is, if I have:

```
$ docker run --rm znc ls -ld /var/lib/znc
drwxr-x---    2 znc      znc              6 Oct 10 03:20 /var/lib/znc
```

Then if I mount a volume on that directory, it will inherit the ownership and permissions:

```
$ docker run --rm -v zncdata:/var/lib/znc znc ls -ld /var/lib/znc
drwxr-x---    2 znc      znc              6 Oct 10 03:20 /var/lib/znc
```

On the other hand, `podman` ignores the permissions on the target directory. Compare to the above:

```
$ podman run --rm znc ls -ld /var/lib/znc
drwxr-x---    2 znc      znc           4096 Oct 10 03:06 /var/lib/znc
$ podman run --rm -v zncdata:/var/lib/znc znc ls -ld /var/lib/znc
drwxr-x---    2 root     root          4096 Oct 10 03:33 /var/lib/znc
```

The behavior is the same for both root and rootless podman.

This makes it very difficult to work with containers that expect to run a service as an unprivileged user: there's no simple way to mount a volume into the container and have it writable by the service.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected mounted volumes to inherit the ownership and permissions of the mountpoint.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Wed Sep 30 15:51:15 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 1
  swapFree: 16841699328
```

```
podman-2.1.1-3.fc31.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes? This is from Fedora's `updates-testing` repository.

comments.body: Can't reproduce. Must have been smoking something.

For the record:

I suspect what happened here is that a I had pre-existing `zncdata` volume (owned by `root`), so podman wasn't actually creating the volume in the above example.
